titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Caribbean and Latin American Postcard Collection
Caribbean and Latin American Postcard Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Jana Krentz and Matthew Gorham

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 10:29

did.unittitle: Caribbean and Latin American postcard collection
did.abstract: This collection consists of postcards and photos from 1890 to 1920 from countries and territories in Latin America and the Caribbean, including Barbados, Bermuda, Cuba, Jamaica, Martinique, Puerto Rico, Panama, Trinidad and Tobago, Venezuela, and Madeira. It also includes a small number of postcards depicting Gibraltar, photographs of the building of the Panama Canal, and artistic postcards from Chile published in 1984.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of postcards and photos from 1890 to 1920 from countries and territories in Latin America and the Caribbean, including Barbados, Bermuda, Cuba, Jamaica, Martinique, Puerto Rico, Panama, Trinidad and Tobago, Venezuela, and Madeira. It also includes a small number of postcards depicting Gibraltar, photographs of the building of the Panama Canal, and artistic postcards from Chile published in 1984.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Caribean and Latin American Postcard Collection (MS 2020). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by country.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Transferred from the Latin American Collection Curator, 2015.

did.unittitle: Santiago de Cuba, Buildings of City
St. Pierre, Martinique. Interior English Cathedral before destruction, 1902
Ruins of St. Pierre, Martinique Island
Y.M.C.A. Buildings, Panama Canal Zone and Other Buildings
